meta.tagline: Award-Winning Moving Company in Detroit, MI
meta.author: Aleksandar Spasevski
meta.doc_subtitle: Walled Lake HQ pin + Detroit Woodward GBP claim + Rochester GBP claim + organic recovery + earned-channel rebuild.

headline_panel.headline: Traffic is dropping. Leads from the GBP→site path are at an 18-month high — and converting at ~2× organic.
headline_panel.lede: Organic search clicks collapsed −74% Aug 2025 → May 2026, but on-site lead volume held through the cliff. Visitors who arrive via the GBP "Website" button submit quote forms at ~17.5% vs 9.2% for organic, and that subset never dipped. The Q3 plan compounds the channel that's already converting — claim 2 more managed GBP pins, surgery the templated burb cohort that triggered the demotion, and rebuild the earned-link channel that the automated stack hollowed out.

stat_tiles.label: GBP→site form_submit/mo (Q1 → Q2 2026*)
stat_tiles.sub: 20/mo (Q1 2026 avg) → 26/mo (Q2 2026* — May full-month projection from 28-day partial). 18-mo high on a monthly basis.

stat_tiles.label: GBP→site form-submit rate (Q2 2026*)
stat_tiles.sub: vs organic 9.2% — GBP click-throughs convert at ~2× the organic rate and held flat through the cliff

stat_tiles.label: On-site lead events/mo (all channels)
stat_tiles.sub: GA4 form_submit firing at ~130/mo across channels (Mar–Apr 2026 run rate, down from ~180/mo in mid-2025 — see Jan–Feb tracking-anomaly callout). Phone-link + GBP-link click events not yet deployed — small GTM/GA4 add to mirror the AQMS event stack.

callout_strip.label: What 'leads' counts here
callout_strip.detail: GA4 form_submit events on the website (~130/mo across channels at current Mar–Apr 2026 run rate). Mirrors the AQMS lead-counting pattern except PM's GA4 only has form_submit firing today — adding click_phone_number + gmb_click events captures the rest of the on-site lead-intent signals. Off-site GBP actions (calls/directions from the listing itself) are out of scope for this measurement layer.

callout_strip.label: Where the leads live now
callout_strip.detail: GBP + Organic + Direct each carry ~25–40% of form_submit volume in any given month. The GBP→site slice is the only one that never dipped — and now leads on conversion rate.

callout_strip.label: What '−74% impressions' hides
callout_strip.detail: Service pages still produce 43% of impressions and 50% of clicks. The cliff was concentrated in the templated burb cohort + long-tail wipeout, not the converting core.

callout_strip.label: Why Lever A compounds
callout_strip.detail: GBP→site click-throughs convert at ~2× organic. PM operates 1 of 3 available pins — claiming Detroit Woodward + Rochester GBPs roughly doubles the converting surface area at no incremental ad spend.

callout_strip.label: Jan–Feb 2026 form_submit anomaly — investigating
callout_strip.detail: Organic + Direct CVR both dropped 40–50% Dec 2025 → Feb 2026 while GBP CVR held steady (~14%). Cross-channel pattern is tracking-signature, not demand: an algorithm or seasonality story would hit organic only; real traffic-mix shifts would hit GBP too. Recovery began March 2026. Likely a GTM/CMS/thank-you-page change in the Dec 20 2025 – Feb 1 2026 window broke form_submit firing for non-GBP-tagged paths. Adds a diagnostic line to the P0 measurement work: pull GTM container history + check form selector / TYP-URL changes in that window. Until confirmed, Q1 YoY CVR (-6%) and 6-month CVR comparisons are partly artifact, not real demand loss.

kpis.label: Maps reach by metro coord
kpis.detail: Top-20 at Walled Lake (1.0), Novi (2.0), Detroit Woodward (2.7 pin-bleed). Zero at Rochester / Royal Oak / Birmingham / Farmington Hills / Plymouth.

kpis.label: LD grid avg rank
kpis.detail: Across 906 cell-appearances / 6 kws (May 1). #3 in market by rank quality (Bos 6.5, Moving Made Easy 7.2, PM 7.9, Two Men 9.6).

kpis.label: Offices vs managed GBPs
kpis.detail: Walled Lake HQ managed. Rochester (419 Golf View Ln) + Detroit Woodward (1001 Woodward Ave) published on site but unmanaged.

kpis.label: Organic impressions, 12mo
kpis.detail: GSC monthly imp 319K (Aug 2025) → 82K (May 2026). Long-tail (51+) wiped 435 → 3 in Ahrefs.

kpis.label: Ship-ready keyword band
kpis.detail: DFS Labs Ranked Keywords: 45 at 1–10, 83 at 11–20, 258 at 21–50, 196 at 51+. The 256 in 11–50 is Lever B's primary target.

kpis.label: Backlinks · form_submit/mo
kpis.detail: DFS Backlinks: 990 total, 247 ref domains, 17 Telegram-spam anchors. GA4 form_submit fires ~130/mo across channels (Mar–Apr 2026; was ~180/mo mid-2025 — see Jan–Feb anomaly). Phone-link + GBP-link click events not yet deployed — small GTM/GA4 add.

trend.headline: Traffic dropping, leads stable and growing
trend.annotation_text: Organic traffic fell 32% Q2 2025 → Q1 2026 (709 → 484 sessions/mo) while GBP→site form_submit volume held flat and is now growing. Lead lines on this chart show GA4 form_submit events (the only conversion event currently firing on PM). Phone-link click + GBP-link click events not yet deployed — small GTM/GA4 add to match the AQMS lead-counting pattern.
trend.may_extrapolation_note: May 2026 conservatively extrapolated: 20 days actual + 11 days at trailing-12-month historical daily rate.

yoy_growth.headline: YoY change vs Q2 2025 — current state + full-quarter estimate
yoy_growth.current_definition: April 2026 actual + May 2026 with last 11 days extrapolated at trailing-12-month daily rate (state as of 2026-05-21). 2-month quarterly average through 5/31. Does NOT include June.
yoy_growth.estimate_definition: Same April + May, plus June 2026 projected at trailing-12-month daily rate. 3-month average — the full-Q projection.
yoy_growth.reading: Organic metrics down 15–25% YoY in both views; GBP metrics up 11–53% YoY. The full-Q estimate widens the GBP wins (June added at trailing-12-month rate compounds May's surge) and slightly worsens the organic picture (May's recovery flattens out).

ga4_gsc_discrepancy.headline: GA4 and GSC tell different stories. Both are right — they measure different things.
ga4_gsc_discrepancy.lede: GSC says PM's organic-search clicks fell 74% in the last 12 months. GA4 says PM's 'Organic Search' channel is 2–4× larger than GSC reports. Neither is wrong. GSC measures clicks on Google's search results page; GA4 measures sessions on the site, however the user arrived. Four structural gaps account for the divergence — and one of them (GBP click-through) is the reason GBP looks so strong in GA4 but invisible in GSC.

comparison_rows.reason: GA4 'Organic Search' channel includes Bing/DuckDuckGo/Yahoo + GBP click-through (gmb/click). GSC is Google Search clicks only.
Same structural reasons; pattern is consistent.
Ratio stable across all windows — divergence is structural, not seasonal.
GBP 'Click to Website' bypasses Google Search results entirely. Only the UTM (?utm_source=gmb) captures it in GA4.
Even Google-only, GA4 > GSC: multi-page sessions, same-day repeat visits, sampling/bot-filter differences, and GA4 session-attribution windowing.
Direct is GA4-only. Likely contains brand-recall traffic + some bot residue (Sept–Oct 2025 spike to 16K/mo coincided with ATB tool active in Asana per Off-Page Risk Assessment §8).
form_submit fires correctly. P0 task flags it as a conversion event in property settings (5-min) and deploys click_phone_number + gmb_click events to mirror the AQMS lead-counting stack.

structural_reasons.title: GBP click-through (the big one)
structural_reasons.detail: GBP 'Click to website' button → utm_source=gmb&utm_medium=click → GA4 buckets these into 'Organic Search'. GSC never sees them because they bypass Google Search. ~149/mo last 30d, ~421/mo last 90d, ~543/yr — fastest-growing slice of GA4 Organic.

structural_reasons.title: Non-Google search engines
structural_reasons.detail: Bing (50 sess 30d), DuckDuckGo (42), Yahoo (42), MSN (9). ~134 sessions/mo in 'Organic Search' that GSC by definition cannot report.

structural_reasons.title: Sessions ≠ clicks (the GA4 vs GSC unit gap)
structural_reasons.detail: One GSC click → one Google Search result click. One GA4 session → can contain multiple pageviews, lasts 30 min of inactivity. Same-day repeat searches from the same user count as separate GSC clicks but one GA4 session. The 1.5× google-only gap is mostly this.

structural_reasons.title: Sampling + bot-filter divergence
structural_reasons.detail: GA4 has its own bot/spam filter applied at the property level. GSC has its own (independent) filter. Neither is documented in full. Small residual gap is unavoidable; tracking month-over-month change is reliable, absolute parity is not.

trust_matrix.question: How much organic search traffic do we get?
trust_matrix.why: GA4 includes all engines + measures sessions which approximate real visits. Use GA4 'Organic Search' channel for traffic-volume reporting.

trust_matrix.question: How is the site ranking on Google?
trust_matrix.why: Position, impressions, clicks, CTR per query and per page only exist in GSC. Use GSC for rank diagnostics.

trust_matrix.question: What's converting?
trust_matrix.why: Conversion events, channel attribution, form_submit live in GA4. For PM today this means form_submit only; M1 task adds click_phone_number + gmb_click events to mirror the AQMS lead stack.

trust_matrix.question: How much traffic is GBP driving to the site?
trust_matrix.why: Only GA4 sees the GBP→site click-through via the UTM. GSC can't measure it. Among those click-throughs, form_submit rate is ~17.5% (Q2 2026* partial) vs organic 9.2% — GBP click-throughs convert at ~2× the organic rate.

trust_matrix.question: Is Google search visibility improving or declining?
trust_matrix.why: GSC is the cleanest signal for SERP-side health — impressions, position, click-loss. Use GSC for the cliff diagnosis.

key_takeaways: The −74% organic-impression decline in GSC is real and worth diagnosing — but it does not mean a −74% revenue cliff. GA4 shows organic-search sessions down ~32% Q2 2025 → Q1 2026, and form_submit volume down only ~17%.
The GBP channel is invisible to GSC by design — every report that draws traffic conclusions from GSC alone undercounts PM's true reach by 30–40%.
When client meetings reference 'organic traffic,' specify which: GSC clicks (Google Search-only) ≠ GA4 Organic Search sessions (all engines + GBP→site). The numbers will always look ~2× different.

Conversion data on this dashboard is the GA4 form_submit event. M1 task adds click_phone_number + gmb_click events to mirror the AQMS lead-counting stack and surface phone-link + GBP-link click intent.
Both should be reconciled in the monthly report. The mini-report linked below contains the full cross-reference table + a method for triangulating channel-level attribution.

ld_leaderboard.name: Two Men and a Truck Moving

ld_timeseries.headline: Maps gained on Detroit-side queries and held strong defense on the volume queries
ld_timeseries.lede: Six priority queries scanned across the 13×13 LD grid. Headline: PM extended top-10 footprint on 'movers detroit' from 9 cells to 67 cells (+58 cells, +1.93 avg-rank positions gained) and doubled top-10 reach on 'detroit moving company' (8 → 16 cells). On the volume queries ('movers', 'movers near me', 'moving companies', 'moving company near me') PM held a top-3 footprint of 64–84 cells out of 121 with essentially flat avg rank through the cliff.

per_keyword_series.headline: Avg rank improved 12.47 → 10.54 (+1.93 positions gained) · top-10 cells 9 → 67
Avg rank improved 14.26 → 13.54 (+0.72 positions gained) · top-10 cells 8 → 16
Strongest defense · avg 3.30 → 3.40 held · top-3 cells 82 → 84

implications: PM's biggest single Maps move this quarter is on 'movers detroit' — top-10 reach grew from 9 cells to 67 cells (7×), with avg rank improving 12.47 → 10.54 (+1.93 positions). The Walled Lake pin is reaching into Detroit-side cells at a rate that puts a managed Detroit Woodward GBP in position to convert this into top-3 placements.

On 'detroit moving company,' top-10 reach doubled (8 → 16 cells) and avg rank improved 14.26 → 13.54. Combined with the 'movers detroit' gain, this is the geometry-extension story Lever A compounds further with a managed Detroit Woodward pin.

On the 4 'volume' queries (movers, movers near me, moving companies, moving company near me), PM held a top-3 footprint of 64–84 cells across the 13×13 grid throughout Feb–May 2026 with avg rank within ±0.30 of baseline — no observable algorithmic damage on the Maps side.

PM has a second indexed listing already showing on 'detroit moving company' at 1001 Woodward Ave (currently unmanaged, ranks ~19). Claiming it is the cleanest path to consolidating PM's Detroit footprint into a single managed entity.

brand_pull.note: Brand carries 54% of clicks from 12% of impressions — classic brand-pull pattern. Non-brand CTR collapsed to 0.09%.

llm_visibility.headline: AI Overview saturation on PM's keyword set: 35% (7/20). PM cited in zero AIOs. YouTube + Reddit dominate.
llm_visibility.lede: Live DataForSEO SERP pull (2026-05-22) on 20 priority PM keywords spanning commercial geo / commercial service / informational / mid-funnel. Two findings drive the Q3 strategy. (1) Commercial geo + service queries DO NOT trigger AI Overviews yet — Google still shows the Local Pack (12/12 commercial queries). (2) Informational queries DO trigger AIOs (5/5 'how to' queries), but PM blog content is not cited; YouTube (18 citations) and Reddit (7) dominate. Mid-funnel cost queries are the migration front — `moving cost detroit` + `how much to tip movers` now have AIOs.

top_cited_domains_in_aio.note: Dominates informational moving content — video format wins
Community Q&A — high authority signal for 'how to' / 'how much' queries
Moving brand — possible content-pattern study target
Single-citation small mover — proves it's possible

implications: **AIO is not yet at commercial-geo + commercial-service queries.** 12 of 12 commercial queries still surface Local Pack. Lever A (GBP claims + DD pins) is the right lever for these — they're still local-pack-dominated SERPs.

**Mid-funnel commercial queries are the migration front.** `moving cost detroit` and `how much to tip movers` already have AIOs. Lever D (BLG-04/05/06) is the timing-correct response — produce AIO-targetable cost + reviews + tipping content before competitors capture the citations.

**Informational queries already migrated.** All 5 'how to' queries return AIO. PM's existing blog (4,884 imp on how-to-pack-dishes, 2,561 on pool-table, etc.) isn't cited because the format doesn't match what AIOs cite — long-form authority pieces, video, or community Q&A.

**YouTube + Reddit dominance is the content-pattern signal.** AIOs heavily cite video and community Q&A. BLG-01..03 refresh should add YouTube embeds + Reddit-style Q&A sections to match the parsing pattern.

**Two competitors cited in AIO** (Allied + Two Men and a Truck on pool-table; Cool Hand Movers on tipping). Content-pattern study target — see how their pages got cited, replicate on BLG-08..10 niche-service cost replicas.
**Zero PM citations across 20 queries** is the baseline. Target: ≥3 citations by end of M3 via BLG-01..06 refresh + new content (Q3 LLM-visibility KPI).

levers.name: Conversion tracking restoration
levers.why: PM's GA4 fires form_submit ~190/mo across channels but the property-settings flag was never set, so GA4 native reports show 0 conversions. AQMS's GA4 stack — the reference implementation — fires four lead-intent events (form_submit, click_phone_number, gmb_click, contact_form_submit). PM has only form_submit firing. M1 closes the property-settings flag and deploys the missing click events via GTM so PM's leads picture matches the AQMS pattern.

actions.name: Mark form_submit as conversion event in GA4 property settings
Deploy click_phone_number + gmb_click GTM events on the website; mark as conversion events in GA4
Verify GBP UTM ?utm_source=gmb fires into GA4
Build Q3 reporting baseline using full GA4 lead-event stack
First monthly report with composite leads metric across all events
Q3 to Q4 quarterly comparison frame

levers.outcome: PM's GA4 lead-event stack matches AQMS's reference pattern. Composite leads metric available per channel and per pin for all subsequent reporting.

levers.name: Map presence expansion
levers.why: PM has Maps reach in 3 of 8 tested metro coords. Two managed-GBP claims (Detroit Woodward + Rochester) plus 5 Driving Direction pins close the geometric ceiling that a single Walled Lake pin can't.

levers.outcome: 3/8 coords with PM in top-3 → 6/8 (M3 target). Detroit Woodward → #1 within 30d of claim. Rochester → top-5 within 60d.

snapshot.eyebrow: HQDM Search Intelligence · Prepared by Aleksandar Spasevski
snapshot.title: Professional Movers.com
snapshot.subtitle: Q3 2026 · Walled Lake, MI · Client Snapshot
snapshot.footer: Client snapshot · Q3 2026 · Prepared by Aleksandar Spasevski
snapshot.exec_html: Your Maps position is the channel carrying the business. Across the 13×13 Detroit-metro grid you hold an average rank of #3–#4 on “movers” and “movers near me”, with 79–84 of 121 grid cells in top-3 — defended cleanly through the year. On Detroit-side queries you’ve extended top-10 reach 7× on “movers detroit” (9 → 67 cells) and doubled it on “detroit moving company” (8 → 16). The Google-Business website button is your highest-converting channel by a wide margin — ~17.5% quote-form rate, nearly 2× the organic baseline — and it’s at an 18-month high. Site-wide conversion rate is up +6% over the last 90 days vs the prior 90 — the recovery curve is in. The Q3 plan compounds what’s already working: claim two more managed Google Business pins (Detroit Woodward + Rochester), tighten the city pages, and rebuild the earned-link channel.

line.title: Organic Performance · Monthly (Jan 2025 → now)
line.caption: Organic visitors (blue, left) and quote-form leads (green, right). 17-month view: peak through spring/summer 2025, then a soft window after the September algorithm update, with lead volume recovering through March 2026 and holding the Mar–Apr range. The dashed tail marks the partial current month.

snapshot.boxes_reading_html: The last 90 days are showing visitors +8%, leads +14%, and conversion rate +6% vs the prior 90 — the recovery curve is in. The Google-Business lead path is at an 18-month high, and Q3 leans into the channel already converting.

focus.title: Expand Maps presence
focus.body: Claim + manage the Detroit Woodward and Rochester Google Business pins and add Driving-Direction pins to close the metros a single Walled Lake pin can’t reach.

focus.title: Page surgery on city pages
focus.body: Clean up the templated suburb pages that triggered the ranking demotion, consolidate thin pages, and rebuild the ones worth keeping with real depth.

focus.title: Rebuild the earned-link profile
focus.body: Replace the hollowed-out automated link stack with local PR, citations, and genuine local links to restore authority.

focus.title: Content + conversion tracking
focus.body: Lock down conversion tracking, refresh top blog posts, and add commercial-intent articles that feed the quote form.

maps.title: Maps Movement · Top Keywords
maps.note_html: Top-3 defense held on the volume queries (64–84 of 121 cells across “movers / movers near me / moving companies”). The offense story is on Detroit-side queries — top-10 reach on “movers detroit” expanded 7× (9 → 67 cells) and “detroit moving company” doubled (8 → 16). Q3 plan converts that reach into top-3 via a managed Detroit Woodward pin.

competitors.note_html: By top-3 footprint you're #2 in the market (behind Two Men & a Truck); by average rank you're #3 (Bos and Moving Made Easy rank tighter). The one to watch is Affordable Movers (+59). Lower avg rank is better.
